name: gpu_vram_read
title: GPU memory read bandwidth
short_description: Read bandwidth from the graphics processor's own memory.

structured_text.description: Reads large buffers out of the graphics processor's local memory with a read only pattern, using a working set far larger than any on chip cache. The result shows how fast the GPU can pull data in from its own memory to feed the compute units.
structured_text.metric_explanation: Bandwidth is the number of bytes read from GPU memory per second, reported in GB/s.
structured_text.value_interpretation: Higher is better. Read bandwidth limits workloads that stream large textures, models and data sets into the GPU faster than they compute.
structured_text.reasoning: Reading from local memory is the most common GPU access pattern, so read bandwidth is the headline figure for data heavy accelerated work.

domain_gaming.explanation: Reading textures and buffers into the cores depends on fast GPU memory reads.

domain_office.explanation: Office work reads little data through GPU memory.

domain_software_development.explanation: Mostly relevant when developing GPU accelerated software.

domain_graphics_design.explanation: Reading large image layers into the GPU is read bandwidth bound.

domain_video_editing.explanation: Reading high resolution frames into the GPU draws on memory read bandwidth.

domain_3d_rendering.explanation: Reading large scenes and textures makes GPU rendering read bandwidth bound.

domain_audio_production.explanation: Audio work reads little through GPU memory.

domain_cad_engineering.explanation: Reading large meshes into the viewport uses GPU read bandwidth.

domain_scientific_computing.explanation: Accelerated simulations are often limited by reads from GPU memory.

domain_ai_training.explanation: Reading weights and activations dominates training memory traffic.

domain_ai_inference.explanation: Token generation speed is largely set by how fast weights are read from GPU memory.

domain_data_science.explanation: GPU analytics scan large arrays out of memory.

domain_server_virt.explanation: Shared GPU servers divide read bandwidth across many tenants.
